name: nr-util
documentation:
# nr.util

General purpose Python utility library.

Compatible with Python 3.7 and higher.

modules.name: nr.util.awsgi
documentation: Utilities for implement ASGI/WSGI applications.

classes.name: nr.util.awsgi._app.App

methods.name: nr.util.awsgi._app.App.initialize
documentation: Delayed initializer, called after the app is handed to the ASGI/WSGI runner to avoid pickling errors.

Base for ASGI/WSGI applications. The class instance itself is not the ASGI/WSGI app, but instead it is expected to
provide that object after and initialization procedure. An #App subclass should encapsulate all logic for the
entirety of the application.

classes.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.gunicorn.GunicornRunner
documentation:
Runs a WSGI application using [Gunicorn][0].

[0]: https://gunicorn.org/

classes.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.uvicorn.UvicornLauncher
documentation: Launches your ASGI/WSGI application via Uvicorn.

modules.name: nr.util.keyvalue
documentation: Provides a simplistic API and a couple of implementations for key/value databases.

modules.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher
documentation: ASGI/WSGI application launchers.

functions.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.launch
documentation:
Launches the application using the specified ASGI/WSGI launcher. If an #App instance is specified, it will be
pickled in this process and unpickled in subprocesses to avoid limitations of finding an application just by
an entrypoint.

functions.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher._pickled_app
documentation: Internal. Loader for a pickled app.

classes.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.AWSGILauncher
documentation: Interface for AWSGI/WSGI application launchers.

functions.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.flask.load_entrypoint
documentation: Loads an entrypoint definition of the form `<module>:<member>`.

classes.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.flask.FlaskRunner

methods.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.flask.FlaskRunner.get_status
documentation:
Returns the status of the application, i.e. whether it is currently running or not. This
method is only relevant when the application was started in the background.

methods.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.flask.FlaskRunner.is_main
documentation:
Checks whether this is the main process, which can be the reloader thread in case
the #FlaskRunner is used.

methods.name: nr.util.awsgi.launcher.flask.FlaskRunner._run
documentation: Internal function to actually invoke the Flask application after everything was prepared.

Runs a #flask.Flask application using the Flask development server. This is not suitable
for production, though very useful during development due to the built-in debugging
capabilities.

Note: Daemonizing the Flask runner is currently only available on systems that support
#os.fork().

classes.name: nr.util.keyvalue._api.Transaction
documentation: Represents a transaction object that can be commited or aborted.

classes.name: nr.util.keyvalue._api.KeyValueStore
documentation: Interface for key-value stores.

classes.name: nr.util.keyvalue._mappingadapter.MappingAdapter
documentation:
Adapter for interacting with a #KeyValueStore as a #Mapping. The default constructor supports plain datatypes
as values to represent an encoder/decoder.

classes.name: nr.util.io._readers.EncodedStreamReader
documentation:
A readable file-like object that wraps a #TextIO object and converts it to a binary stream encoded with the specified
*encoding* (or the default system encoding).

Example:

```py
import io
from nr.utils.io.readers import EncodedStreamReader
fp = EncodedStreamReader(io.StringIO('äöü'), 'utf-8')
assert fp.read(1) == b'Ã'
assert fp.read(1) == b'¤'
```

classes.name: nr.util.atomic._counter.AtomicCounter

methods.name: nr.util.atomic._counter.AtomicCounter.get
documentation: Get the current value of the counter.

methods.name: nr.util.atomic._counter.AtomicCounter.inc
documentation: Increment the counter by one.

methods.name: nr.util.atomic._counter.AtomicCounter.dec
documentation: Decrement the counter by one. Raises a #ValueError if attempting to decrement a value if zero.

methods.name: nr.util.atomic._counter.AtomicCounter.join
documentation: Block until the counter reaches zero, or until the timeout expires.
A thread-safe counter.

functions.name: nr.util.text._substitute_ranges.substitute_ranges
documentation:
Replaces parts of *text* using the specified *ranges* and returns the new text. Ranges must not overlap.
*is_sorted* can be set to `True` if the input *ranges* are already sorted from lowest to highest starting index to
optimize the function.

modules.name: nr.util.keyvalue.sqlite
documentation: A key-value store implementation based on sqlite3.

classes.name: nr.util.keyvalue.sqlite.SqliteDatastore

methods.name: nr.util.keyvalue.sqlite.SqliteDatastore.get_namespaces
documentation:
Returns an iterator that returns the name of all namespaces known to the Sqlite store. Note
that new namespaces are created on-deman using #store().

methods.name: nr.util.keyvalue.sqlite.SqliteDatastore.get_keys
documentation:
Returns an iterator that returns all keys in the specified *namespace* and their expiration
timestamp. This excludes any keys that are already expired but not yet expunged.

Provider for key-value datastores backed by an SQLite3 database.

The #SqliteDatastore is thread-safe, but may be slow to access concurrently due to locking requirements.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._path.is_relative_to
documentation:
Returns `True` if path *a* is relative to path *b*. A backfill for #Path.is_relative_to() for Python versions
older than 3.9.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._discovery.get_file_in_directory
documentation: Returns a file in *directory* that is either in the *preferred* list or starts with specified *prefix*.

modules.name: nr.util.fs
documentation: Utilities for filesystem operations.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._chmod.flags
documentation: Modifies the stat flags according to *modstring*, mirroring the syntax for POSIX `chmod`.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._chmod.repr
documentation: Returns a string representation of the access flags *flags*.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._chmod.update
documentation: Updates the mode of the *path* in a `chmod` like fashion.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._atomic.atomic_write
documentation:
Write to a temporarily file, then rename on file closure. If an error occurs while the context manager is active,
the temporary file will be deleted instead and if an original file existed before it will not be modified. On
Windows systems, the file cannot be replaced in an atomic operation, so it will need to be deleted first.

functions.name: nr.util.fs._atomic.atomic_swap
documentation:
Similar to #atomic_write(), only that this function writes to the *path* directlty instead of a temporary file,
and save the original version of the file next to it in the same directory by temporarily renaming it. If the context
exits without error, the old file will be removed. Otherwise, the new file will be deleted and the old file will be
renamed back to *path*. If *always_revert* is enabled, the original file will be restored even if the context exits
without errors.

modules.name: nr.util.proxy
documentation:
Provides classes that proxy all operations to a delegate object, allowing to implement behaviours at runtime where
the object contained in a "variable" can be interchanged or different based on the async context or thread.

classes.name: nr.util.proxy._contextlocal.ContextLocalProxy

methods.name: nr.util.proxy._contextlocal.ContextLocalProxy.__init__
documentation:
Create a new thread-local proxy. If an *error_message* is provided, it will be the
message of the #RuntimeError that will be raised if the thread local is accessed
without being initialized in the same thread first.
A proxy that stores a stack of objects in a #contextvars.ContextVar (for async programs).

functions.name: nr.util.proxy._proxy.bind
documentation:
(Re-) bind the function for a proxy. The *func* will be called in the future when the current value of the
proxy is required.

functions.name: nr.util.proxy._proxy.proxy
documentation:
Create a new proxy from the given supplier. The function is typed to return an instance of the generic type
*T*, but will in fact return a #Proxy object.

classes.name: nr.util.proxy._proxy.Proxy

methods.name: nr.util.proxy._proxy.Proxy.__init__
documentation:
Create a new proxy object.

# Arguments
func (callable): The function that will be called to retrieve the object represented by the proxy when demanded.
  This will be called again and again for every operation performed on the proxy, unless #lazy is enabled. If no
  func is set, the proxy is unbound. The proxy can later be bound using the #bind() function.
name (str): The name of the proxy. Can make debugging easier if the proxy is unbound.
lazy (bool): Call the *func* function only once on first use, then cache the result.

methods.name: nr.util.proxy._proxy.Proxy._get_current_object
documentation: proxy_base

Wraps an object returned by a callable. Every time the proxy is accessed, the callable is invoked and the access is
redirected to the proxied object.

> __Note__: For some use cases, using a proxy can lead to problems when `isinstance()` or checks are used. For
> example if you pass a proxy to a function that accepts an iterable, the instancecheck with
> #collections.abc.Iterable will always return `True` as the #Proxy class implements the `__iter__()` method. If
> you are stuck with this problem, use the #make_cls() function to create a new class excluding the `__iter__()`
> method.

classes.name: nr.util.proxy._threadlocal.ThreadLocalProxy

methods.name: nr.util.proxy._threadlocal.ThreadLocalProxy.__init__
documentation:
Create a new thread-local proxy. If an *error_message* is provided, it will be the
message of the #RuntimeError that will be raised if the thread local is accessed
without being initialized in the same thread first.
A proxy that contains a thread-local stack of objects.

methods.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__dict__
documentation: forward special function calls and attribute access

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__delattr__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__str__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__eq__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__ne__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__hash__
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.proxy._base.BaseProxy.__class__
documentation: type: ignore

modules.name: nr.util.url
documentation: Tools for URL handling.

classes.name: nr.util.url.Url

methods.name: nr.util.url.Url.netloc
documentation: Returns the entire network location with auth and port.

methods.name: nr.util.url.Url.auth
documentation: Returns just the auth part of the network location.

methods.name: nr.util.url.Url.netloc_no_auth
documentation: Returns the network location without the auth part.

methods.name: nr.util.url.Url.of
documentation:
Parses the *url* string into its parts.

Raises:
  ValueError: If an invalid URL is passed (for example if the port number cannot be parsed to an integer).
Helper to represent the components of a URL, including first class support for username, password, host and port.

modules.name: nr.util.exceptions
documentation: Utils for implement and dealing with exceptions.

functions.name: nr.util.exceptions.safe_str
documentation:
Decorator for a #__str__() method of an #Exception subclass that catches an exception that occurs in the
string formatting function, logs it and returns the message of the occurred exception instead.

methods.name: nr.util.process._pidfile.Pidfile.set_pid
documentation: Writes the *pid* into the file.

methods.name: nr.util.process._pidfile.Pidfile.get_pid
documentation:
Returns the PID saved in the file. May raise a #FileNotFounError if the file does not
exist or a #ValueError if the file does not contain an integer.

methods.name: nr.util.process._pidfile.Pidfile.get_status
documentation: Determines the #Status of a process.

methods.name: nr.util.process._pidfile.Pidfile.stop
documentation:
Stops the process referenced by the PID file. If the file does not exist or the
process is not alive, this is a no-op.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.process_exists
documentation:
Checks if the processed with the given *pid* exists. Returns #True if
that is the case, #False otherwise.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.process_terminate
documentation:
Terminates the process with the given *pid*. First sends #signal.SIGINT,
followed by #signal.SIGTERM after *timeout* seconds, followed by
#signal.SIGKILL after *timeout* seconds if the process has not responded to
the terminate signal.

The fallback to kill can be disabled by setting *allow_kill* to False.
Returns True if the process was successfully terminated or killed, or if
the process did not exist in the first place.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.getpwgrnam
documentation:
A combination of #pwd.getpwnam() and #pwd.getgrnam(), where *group*,
if specified, overrides the group ID of the *user*. Returns a tuple of
the user's home folder, the user ID and the group ID.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.replace_stdio
documentation:
Replaces the file handles of stdin/sdout/stderr, closing the original
file descriptors if necessary.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.detach
documentation:
Detaches the current process from the parent process. This function
requires #os.setsid() and thus works only on Unix systems.

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.spawn_fork
documentation:
Spawns a single fork process and calls *func*. If *detach* is #True,
the fork will be detached first (note that this process will still be killed
by it's parent process if it doesn't exist gracefully).

This is useful if *func* spawns another process, which will then behave like
a daemon (as it will NOT be killed if the original process dies).

functions.name: nr.util.process._utils.spawn_daemon
documentation:
Spawns a daemon process that runs *func*. This performs two forks to
avoid the parent process killing the process that runs *func*.

Note that this is only needed if you want to run a Python function as a
daemon. If you were to spawn another process that should act as a daemon,
you only need to fork once as the subprocess will then be the second "fork".

modules.name: nr.util.config
documentation:
Helpers for loading configurations and in-memory configuration state.

To make use of this module, you need `PyYAML` and `databind.json` installed.

modules.name: nr.util.logging.formatters.terminal_colors
documentation: Provides a logging formatter that understands color hints in the message and decorates it with

classes.name: nr.util.logging.formatters.terminal_colors.TerminalColorFormatter

methods.name: nr.util.logging.formatters.terminal_colors.TerminalColorFormatter.install
documentation:
Install the formatter on stream handlers on all handlers of the root logger that are attached to a TTY,
or otherwise on all that are not attached to a TTY based on the *target* value. If no value is specified, it
will install into TTY-attached stream handlers if #styles is set.

A formatter that enhances text decorated with HTML-style tags with ANSI terminal colors. It can also be
configured to eliminate the HTML tags instead of converting them to terminal styles.

modules.name: nr.util.re
documentation: Utility functions for regular expressions. Builds on top of the standard library #re module.

functions.name: nr.util.re.match_full
documentation:
Matches *expr* from the start of *string* and expects that it can be matched throughout.
If it fails to consume the full string, a #MatchAllError will be raised.

classes.name: nr.util.re.MatchFullError
documentation: Raised when #match_full() cannot consume the full string.

functions.name: nr.util.process.root.elevate
documentation:
Runs a command as an admin in the specified *cwd* and *environ*. On Windows, this creates a temporary directory where
this information is stored temporarily so that the new process can launch the proper subprocess.

variables.name: nr.util.process.root.windll
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: nr.util.process.root.WinError
documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: nr.util.process.root.get_last_error
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: nr.util.date.duration.duration

methods.name: nr.util.date.duration.duration.total_seconds
documentation: Computes the total number of seconds in this duration.

methods.name: nr.util.date.duration.duration.as_timedelta
documentation:
Returns the seconds represented by this duration as a #datetime.timedelta object. The arguments
and keyword arguments are forwarded to the #total_seconds() method.

methods.name: nr.util.date.duration.duration.as_relativedelta
documentation:
Converts the #duration object to a #dateutil.relativedelta.relativedelta object. Requires
the `python-dateutil` module.

methods.name: nr.util.date.duration.duration.parse
documentation:
Parses an ISO 8601 duration string into a #duration object.

Thanks to https://stackoverflow.com/a/35936407.
See also https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/ISO_8601#Durations
Represents an ISO 8601 duration.

methods.name: nr.util.date.format._datetime_format.compile
documentation: Compiles a format string to a static regex representation for fast parsing.

modules.name: nr.util
documentation: General purpose utility library.

classes.name: nr.util.date.format_sets.format_set
documentation:
Format sets represent a group of date, time and dateime formats. When formatting a value, it will
use the first format defined in the group. When parsing, it will attempt to parse the value using
all of the provided formats (stopping on the first successful parse).

#format_datetime() can take into account the #date_formats and #time_formats as well if the
*partial* parameter is set to #True.

modules.name: nr.util.algorithm
documentation: A selection of useful algorithms.

classes.name: nr.util.date.options._FormatOption
documentation:
Represents a special character in a date format string, which in turn represents a particular
date or time component.

classes.name: nr.util.date.options.IFormatOption

methods.name: nr.util.date.options.IFormatOption.parse_string
documentation: Called to parse then value extracted by the *regex*.

methods.name: nr.util.date.options.IFormatOption.format_value
documentation: Called to format the value of the respective #Component to a string.

Abstract metaclass on top of #_FormatOption, adding methods to the interface for parsing and
formatting values represented by the format option.

classes.name: nr.util.date.options.NumericFormatOption
documentation: Default implementation for format options that represent a plain number component in the date.

classes.name: nr.util.date.options.FormatOptions
documentation: Enumeration of all the available format options.

functions.name: nr.util.algorithm.longest_common_substring.longest_common_substring
documentation: Finds the longest common contiguous sequence of elements in *seq1* and *seq2* and returns it.

functions.name: nr.util.inspect.get_callsite
documentation:
Returns information on the callsite of the specified stackdepth. Defaults to 1 to point to the immediate
stack frame above from where the function is called. The returned object contains information on the filename
and line number.

modules.name: nr.util.safearg
documentation:
Utilities to mark values safe or unsafe, for example for the purpose of logging or to expose to users.
Note that anything accepting #Arg instances must explicitly implement support.

classes.name: nr.util.safearg.Arg
documentation: Base class for arguments. Cannot be directly constructed.

classes.name: nr.util.chaindict.ChainDict
documentation:
A dictionary that wraps a list of dictionaries. Except for the first dictionary passed into the #ChainDict
constructor, none will be mutated through any actions on this dictionary.

functions.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._color.parse_color
documentation:
Parses a color string of one of the following formats and returns a corresponding #SgrColor, #LutColor or
#TrueColor.

* `<color_name>`, `BRIGHT_<color_name>`: #SgrColor (case insensitive, underline optional)
* `%rgb`, `$xxx`: #LutColor
* `#cef`, `#cceeff`: #TrueColor

classes.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._color.SgrColor
documentation: Represents a color from the SGR space (see #SgrColorName).

classes.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._color.LutColor

methods.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._color.LutColor.from_rgb
documentation:
Given RGB values in the range of [0..5], returns a #LutColor pointing
to the color index that resembles the specified color coordinates.
Represents a LUT color, which is one of 216 colors.

classes.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._color.TrueColor
documentation: Represents a true color comprised of three color components.

functions.name: nr.util.functional._assure.assure
documentation:
Assures that *v* is not `None` and returns it. If the value is in fact `None`, a {@link ValueError} will
be raised raised.

classes.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._style.Style

methods.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._style.Style.__init__
documentation:
The constructor allows you to specify all arguments also as strings. The foreground and background are parsed
with #parse_color(). The *attrs* can be a comma-separated string.
A style is a combination of foreground and background color, as well as a list of attributes.

classes.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._style.StyleManager

methods.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._style.StyleManager.parse_style
documentation: Parses a style string that is valid inside an opening HTML-style tag accepted in strings by #format().

methods.name: nr.util.terminal.colors._style.StyleManager.format
documentation:
Formats text that contains HTML-style tags that represent styles in the style manager. In addition, special
tags `<fg={color}>`, `<bg={color}>` or <attr={attrs}>` can be used to manually specify the exact styling of the
text and the can be combined (such as `<bg=bright red;attr=underline>`). If *safe* is set to `True`, tags
referencing styles that are unknown to the manager are ignored.
Allows you to register styles and format text using HTML-style tags.

methods.name: nr.util.refreshable.Refreshable.map
documentation:
Map the value of the refreshable to a new refreshable that automatically gets updated when the
parent is updated. Be aware that this method should be used sparingly as it registers a new
subscriber to this refreshable that will never be disposed of again.

functions.name: nr.util.functional._supplier.supplier_get
documentation:
A helper method to invoke a supplier, if *value* is a supplier, or return the value directory. This is
useful in particular for patterns where a static value as well as a supplier is a valid value for the variable.
Note that the type *U* must not be callable, otherwise it is recognized as a supplier.

__Example__

  site_name: Supplier[str | None] | str | None = ...
  value = supplier_get(site_name)

functions.name: nr.util.functional._coalesce.coalesce
documentation:
Returns the first value that is not `None`. If a not-None fallback is specified, the function is guaranteed
to return a not-Non value.

modules.name: nr.util.task
documentation: Provides generalized APIs to orchestrate background tasks.

modules.name: nr.util.plugins._pkg_resources
documentation: Helpers to implement a plugin infrastructure in Python.

functions.name: nr.util.plugins._pkg_resources.load_entrypoint
documentation: Load a single entrypoint value. Raises a #RuntimeError if no such entrypoint exists.

functions.name: nr.util.plugins._pkg_resources.iter_entrypoints
documentation: Loads all entrypoints from the given group.

classes.name: nr.util.plugins._pkg_resources.NoSuchEntrypointError
documentation: NOTE (@NiklasRosenstein): I wished we could use a TypeVar bound to a protocol instead of T, but mypy does
not seem to like it.

classes.name: nr.util.task._api.Runnable
documentation:
Abstract representation of something that can be run, for example in a task. Depending on the
use case, runnables may need to be serializable.

classes.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus.idle
documentation: True if the status is either #PENDING or #QUEUED.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus.running
documentation: True if the status is #RUNNING.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus.done
documentation: True if the status is either #SUCCEEDED, #FAILED or #IGNORED.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus.ignored
documentation: True if the status is #IGNORED.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskStatus.completed
documentation:
Returns `True` if the status represents a "completed" stated, i.e. the task will not change
going forward (except maybe for it's #Task.error_consumed property). The set of completed statuses
is #SUCCEEDED, #FAILED, #CANCELLED and #IGNORED.
Represents the statuses that a task can be in.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskCallbacks.add
documentation:
Add a callback to the task. If the *group* is specified, it can be used to remove the callbacks
from the task wtih #remove_group(). The #condition determines when the *callback* will be invoked.
If it returns `True` at the time when #add() is used, the *callback* is invoked immediately and
will not be added to the collection.

# Arguments
condition: The condition on which to invoke them *callback*.
callback: The callback to invoke when the task status is updated and the *condition* matches.
once: If set to `True`, the callback will be invoked only once and removed from the task after.
group: A string that identifies the group that the callback belongs to.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskCallbacks.remove
documentation: Remove all callbacks from the collection that belong to the specified *group*.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.TaskCallbacks.on
documentation:
Adds *callback* to the collection, which will be invoked if the task status is or changes to one
that is specified via *state*. If *state* is `'end'`, it will match #TaskStatus.SUCCEEDED,
#TaskStatus.FAILED and #TaskStatus.IGNORED. If the *state* is `'start'`, it behaves the same as
# `'end'` but also match #TaskStatus.RUNNING.

classes.name: nr.util.task._api.Executor

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Executor.execute
documentation:
Execute the given *runnable* object in the task manager.

# Arguments
runnable: The runnable to invoke. Depending on the implementation of the task manager, this object
  may need to be serializable with whatever serializer the implementation is using (in case of a
  distributed task manager).
name: An optional name for the runnable. If not specified, the #repr() of the object will be used.
at: The timestamp at which the runnable is supposed to be executed. If the timestamp is smaller than
  the current time or if the parameter is not set, it will be executed immediately.

# Returns
The #Task object for this runnable.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Executor.shutdown
documentation:
Shut down the task manager, preventing new tasks from being exexuted. if *cancel_running_tasks*
is enabled, all currently running tasks will be cancelled. By default, the method blocks until
all running tasks have exited.

This method may raise a #RuntimeError if it was called before.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Executor.join
documentation: Block until all pending and currently running tasks have been processed.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Executor.idlejoin
documentation: Like #join(), but garuantee a call to #shutdown() in the end.
Interface for task managers that can dispatch tasks for execution.

classes.name: nr.util.task._api.Task

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Task.result
documentation:
Return the result value of the task. Raises a `RuntimeError` while the task is still running.
Re-raises the #error if it is set. Returns `None` if the task has been ignored.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Task.cancelled
documentation: Returns `True` if the task has been cancelled.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Task.sleep
documentation:
Sleep for *duration* seconds or until the task is cancelled.

Returns `True` if the sleep completed, `False` is the timeout triggered which means
that the task has been cancelled.

methods.name: nr.util.task._api.Task.join
documentation:
Block until the task completes or until the *timeout* is exceeded. A task is complete when its
status is one of #TaskStatus.SUCCEEDED, #TaskStatus.FAILED or #TaskStatus.IGNORED. Returns `True`
if the join is complete, `False` if the timeout was exceeded.
Abstract representation of a task.

classes.name: nr.util.stream.Stream

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.batch
documentation:
Convert the stream into a stream of batches of size *n*, where each element of the stream
contains the result of the *collector* after passing up to *n* elements of the original
stream into it.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.bipartition
documentation:
Use a predicate to partition items into false and true entries.
Returns a tuple of two streams with the first containing all elements
for which *pred* returned #False and the other containing all elements
where *pred* returned #True.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.call
documentation: Calls every item in *iterable* with the specified arguments.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.collect
documentation: Collects the stream into a collection.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.count
documentation: Returns the number of items in the stream. This fully consumes the stream.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.concat
documentation: Concatenate all values in the stream into a single stream of values.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.consume
documentation: Consume the contents of the stream, up to *n* elements if the argument is specified.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.distinct
documentation:
Yields unique items from *iterable* whilst preserving the original order. If *skip* is
specified, it must be a set or sequence of items to skip in the first place (ie. items to
exclude from the returned stream). The specified set/sequence is modified in-place. Using a
set is highly recommended for performance purposes.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.filter
documentation: Agnostic to Python's built-in `filter()` function.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.first
documentation: Returns the first element of the stream, or `None`.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.firstopt
documentation: Returns the first element of the stream as an `Optional`.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.flatmap
documentation: Same as #map() but flattens the result.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.map
documentation: Agnostic to Python's built-in `map()` function.

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.of_type
documentation: Filters using #isinstance().

methods.name: nr.util.stream.Stream.sortby
documentation:
Creates a new sorted stream. Internally the #sorted() built-in function is used so a new list
will be created temporarily.

# Parameters
by (str, callable): Specify by which dimension to sort the stream. If a string is specified,
  it will be used to retrieve a key or attribute from the values in the stream. In the case of
  a callable, it will be used directly as the `key` argument to #sorted().
A stream is an iterable with utility methods to transform it.

classes.name: nr.util.plugins._plugin_registry.PluginRegistry

methods.name: nr.util.plugins._plugin_registry.PluginRegistry.register
documentation:
Register a plugin under the specified *group* and *name*. If the *plugin* is callable (i.e. a function,
method or lambda), it will be called with no arguments everytime #group() is called with the expectation to
return an actual instance of the plugin. Registering multiple plugins with the same *name* in the same *group*
causes that plugin to be registered multiple times (instead of replacing the previously registered plugin).
A helper class to register plugins associated with a unique identifier and later access them.

modules.name: nr.util.annotations
documentation: A set of helper functions that make it easy to attach arbitrary data to functions or classes.

functions.name: nr.util.annotations.add_annotation
documentation:
Registers an annotation in the given object, grouped under the specified base type. All annotations added to
the object can be retrieved with #get_annotations() or #get_annotation(). If *front* is enabled, the annotation
will be inserted at the front of the group instead of appended to the back.

functions.name: nr.util.annotations.get_annotations
documentation: Returns all annotations registered on the given object in the group of the base type.

functions.name: nr.util.annotations.get_annotation
documentation:
Returns the first annotation registered to the given object in the group of the base type,
or `None` if no annotations are registered for the given base type.

classes.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker

methods.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker.__bool__
documentation: Returns `True` if the walker is pointing to an element in the sequence, `False` if not.

methods.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker.current
documentation: Returns the item in the sequence that the walker is currently pointing to.

methods.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker.has_next
documentation:
Returns `True` if calling {@meth next()} will be successful, i.e. if there is a next element that the walker
can move to.

methods.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker.next
documentation:
Returns the next element in the sequence. Note that this cannot be used to advance the walker beyond the
end of the sequence; use #advance() instead.

methods.name: nr.util.iter._sequence_walker.SequenceWalker.safe_iter
documentation:
A utility iterator that yields the current line of the scanner at each step of the iteration. After each
step, it ensures that the Scanner was at least moved forward by one index, or modified at all if *strict* is
disabled.
The sequence walker is a utility class to walk over the items of a sequence in an easy to use manner.

functions.name: nr.util.preconditions.check_argument
documentation:
Raise a #ValueError if the specified *value* is `False`. The specified *message* will be used as
the message for the produced exception. The *message* may either be a string or an arg-less
function that produces a string.

functions.name: nr.util.preconditions.check_not_none
documentation: Raises a #ValueError if *value* is `None`.

functions.name: nr.util.preconditions.check_instance_of
documentation:
Raises a #TypeError if *value* is not an instance of the specified *types*. If no message is
provided, it will be auto-generated for the given *types*.

functions.name: nr.util.preconditions.check_subclass_of
documentation:
Raises a #TypeError if *cls* is not a subclass of one of the specified *types*. If *cls* is not
a type, a different #TypeError is raised that does not include the specified *message*.

modules.name: nr.util.task._default
documentation: Provides the #DefaultTaskManager which operates locally to run tasks in a pool of threads.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.TaskCallbacks.add
documentation:
Add a callback to the collection. If the *group* is specified, it can be used to remove
the callbacks from the task wtih #remove_group(). The #condition determines when the *callback*
will be invoked. If it returns `True` at the time when #add() is used, the *callback* is invoked
immediately and will not be added to the collection.

# Arguments
condition: The condition on which to invoke them *callback*.
callback: The callback to invoke when the task status is updated and the *condition* matches.
once: If set to `True`, the callback will be invoked only once and removed from the task after.
group: A string that identifies the group that the callback belongs to.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.TaskCallbacks.remove
documentation: Remove all callbacks from the collection that belong to the specified *group*.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.TaskCallbacks._invoke
documentation: Internal. Invokes all callbacks based on their condition.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task._update
documentation:
Update the status of the task. This should be used only by the executor engine where the task
is queued. A status change will immediately invoke the registered #callbacks. Some status
transitions are not allowed (ex. from #api.TaskStatus.SUCCEEDED to #api.TaskStatus.RUNNING). In this
case, a #RuntimeError will be raised. Similarly, if the *status* is #api.TaskStatus.FAILED but
no *error* is given, a #RuntimeError will be raised as well.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.status
documentation: Returns the current status of the task.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.error
documentation:
Returns the exception that occurred while executing the task. This is only set if
the #status is #api.TaskStatus.FAILED.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.error_consumed
documentation: Returns #True if the error in the task is marked as consumed.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.consume_error
documentation:
Mark the error in the task as consumed. This can be called from a callback, but can only be
called if the #status is #api.TaskStatus.FAILED.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.cancel
documentation:
Set the cancelled flag on the task. After calling this method, #cancelled() will return `True`
and any thread that is currently using #sleep() will be immediately woken up (rather than
waiting for the timeout to kick in).

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.cancelled
documentation:
Returns `True` if the task has been cancelled (with the #cancel() methdo). The task subclass
should use this to check if execution should continue or not.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.Task.sleep
documentation:
Sleep for *sec* seconds, or until the task is cancelled with the #cancel() method. This should
be used instead of #time.sleep() inside the task's #run() implementation to ensure quick
task termination.

Returns `True` if the task has been cancelled (saving a subsequent call to #cancelled()).

classes.name: nr.util.task._default.Worker
documentation:
The worker thread accepts tasks from a queue and executes them. Receiving #None from the queue
indicates to the worker that its should stop.

methods.name: nr.util.task._default.DefaultExecutor.execute
documentation:
Queue a task for execution in the worker pool. If there is a free slot in the pool and all
existing workers are busy, a new worker will be spawned immediately.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.extractor.TokenExtractor

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.extractor.TokenExtractor.get_token
documentation: Extract a token value from the current position of the scanner.
Interface for token extraction.

modules.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner
documentation:
Provides the #Scanner class which is convenient for scanning through items of a sequence; such as characters
in a text.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Cursor.get_line_begin
documentation: Returns a cursor pointing to the beginning of the current line.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.pos
documentation: Moves the scanner back (or forward) to the specified cursor location.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.char
documentation: Returns the current character. Returns an empty string at the end of the text.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.seek
documentation:
Moves the cursor of the Scanner to or by *offset* depending on the *mode*. The method is
similar to a file's `seek()` method, but ensures that the line and column counts are tracked
correctly.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.next
documentation: Move on to the next character in the text.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.readline
documentation: Reads a full line from the scanner and returns it.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.match
documentation:
Matches the *regex* from the current character of the *scanner* and returns the result. The
scanners column and line numbers are updated respectively.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.search
documentation:
Performs a regex search from the current position of the scanner. Note that searching in the
scanner will potentially have you skip characters without consuming them.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.getmatch
documentation:
The same as #Scanner.match(), but returns the captured group rather than
the regex match object, or None if the pattern didn't match.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._scanner.Scanner.getline
documentation: Returns the contents of the current line marked by the specified cursor location.
A convenient class for scanning through items of a sequence; such as characters in a text.

classes.name: nr.util.git.Git

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.clone
documentation:
Clone a Git repository to the *to_directory* from *clone_url*. If a relative path is
specified in *to_directory*, it will be treated relative to the #Git.cwd path.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.add
documentation: Add files to the index.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_branches
documentation: Get the branches of the repository. Returns a list of #Branch objects.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_branch_names
documentation: Get the branch names.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_current_branch_name
documentation: Return the name of the current branch.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.push
documentation: Push the specified *refs* to the Git remote.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.pull
documentation: Pull from the specified Git remote.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.fetch
documentation: Fetch a remote repository (or multiple).

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.remotes
documentation: List up all the remotes of the repository.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.add_remote
documentation: Add a remote with the specified name.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_status
documentation: Returns the file status for the working tree.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.commit
documentation: Commit staged files to the repository.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.tag
documentation: Create a tag.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.rev_parse
documentation: Parse a Git ref into a shasum.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.rev_list
documentation: Return a list of all Git revisions, optionally in the specified path.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.has_diff
documentation: Returns #True if the repository has changed files.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.create_branch
documentation: Creates a branch.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.checkout
documentation: Check out the specified ref or files.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.reset
documentation: Reset to the specified ref or reset the files.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_commit_message
documentation: Returns the commit message of the specified *rev*.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_toplevel
documentation: Return the toplevel directory of the Git repository. Returns #None if it does not appear to be a Git repo.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_files
documentation: Returns a list of all the files tracked in the Git repository.

methods.name: nr.util.git.Git.get_file_contents
documentation:
Returns the contents of a file at the given revision. Raises a #FileNotFoundError if the file did not
exist at the revision.
Utility class to interface with the Git commandline.

functions.name: nr.util.parsing.rules.regex
documentation:
Creates a tokenizer rule that matches a regular expression and returns the #re.Match object
as the token value. If you want the pattern to match at the start of a line only, set the
*at_line_start_only* argument to `True`. The regex caret (`^`) control character will not work
because the regex is matched from the cursor's current position and not the line start.

functions.name: nr.util.parsing.rules.regex_extract
documentation:
Creates a token extractor that matches a regular expression and extracts a group from the match
as the token value.

functions.name: nr.util.parsing.rules.string_literal
documentation: Matches a Python string literal.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleSet

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleSet.__init__
documentation: The default constructor for a #RuleSet uses string for both the token and value type.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleSet.__init__
documentation: Initialize the #RuleSet with a sentinel configuration.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleSet.rule
documentation: Add a rule and return self.
A ordered list of parsing rules that is used a the #Tokenizer.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleConfigSet

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.ruleset.RuleConfigSet.set
documentation:
Set the value of one or more token types. The returned context manager _may_ be used, but
does not _have_ to be used, to revert to the previous state.

Implementation detail: strings are not considered collections when identifying the type
of the *token_types* argument.
Helper class to manage values associated with token types.

functions.name: nr.util.digraph.algorithm.topological_sort.topological_sort
documentation:
Calculate the topological order for elements in the *graph*.

@raises RuntimeError: If there is a cycle in the graph.

functions.name: nr.util.digraph.algorithm.remove_with_predecessors.remove_with_predecessors
documentation:
Remove the given nodes from the graph, and their predecessors if they are not inputs to nodes that are kept on
the graph.

classes.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.__init__
documentation: Create a new empty directed graph.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.add_node
documentation:
Add a node to the graph. Overwrites the existing node value if the *node_id* already exists in the graph,
but keeps its edges intact.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.add_edge
nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.nodes
documentation: Returns a view on the nodes in the graph.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.edges
documentation: Returns a view on the edges in the graph.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.roots
documentation: Return the nodes of the graph that have no predecessors.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.leafs
documentation: Return the nodes of the graph that have no successors.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.predecessors
documentation:
Returns a sequence of the given node's predecessor node IDs.

@raises UnknownNodeError: If the node does not exist.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.successors
documentation:
Returns a sequence of the given node's successor node IDs.

@raises UnknownNodeError: If the node does not exist.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph.copy
documentation:
Return a copy of the graph. Note that the data is still the same, which may be undesirable if
it is intended to be mutable.

methods.name: nr.util.digraph._digraph.DiGraph._get_node
documentation: Internal

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Debug
documentation: Flags that enable/disable various debug logs in the tokeninzer.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Token
documentation:
Represents a token extracted by the tokenizer. When the end of the text is reached and no more
tokens can be extracted, the *type* and *value* will be set to what is defined in the #RuleSet
to be used as the sentinel.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.ProxyToken
documentation: Always represents the current token of the tokenizer.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.TokenizerState
documentation: A checkpoint that can be used to restore the tokenizer to a previous state.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.TokenizationError
documentation: Raised when the text cannot be tokenized.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.UnexpectedTokenError
documentation: Raised when the text could only be tokenized into an unexpected token.

classes.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer.state
documentation: The position of the tokenizer. Can be set to go back to a previously stored position.

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer.current
documentation:
Returns the token that was last extracted in the #next() method. Raises an #EOF exception if
the scanner has reached the end of the text. Raises a #RuntimeError if #next() has not been
called successfully at least once. (You can check if the #initialized property returns `False`
to see if the #RuntimeError would be raised).

methods.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer.next
documentation:
Extracts the next token from the text and returns it. Without arguments, all currently
non-ignored rules are considered. You may use the arguments to further reduce the set of
rules used for parsing.

If no token can be extracted using the specified rule subset, the tokenizer will attempt
to tokenize using the remaining set of rules. If the extracted token is skippable, the next
token will be extracted recursively. If the extracted token is not skippable, an
#UnexpectedTokenError is raised.

Note that the order in which rules are checked is always fixed to the order in which they
occur on the rule set.

# Arguments

select (list): A list of token types that will be prioritized for extracting the next token.
  All token types passed into the *select* argument are taken into account as if they were
  also supplied to the *expect* argument.

unselect (list): A list of token types that will be deprioritized for extracting the next
  token. The remaining token types are taken into account as if they were also supplied to the
  *expect* argument. The argument cannot be mixed with *select*.

expect (list): A list of token types that are expected to be returned from this call. If the
  extracted token is not one of the specified token types, an #UnexpectedTokenError is raised.
  Skippable tokens are not skipped if they are expected via this argument.

# Raises

TokenizationError: If no token could be extracted from the current possition of the #scanner.

UnexpectedTokenError: If the extracted token is not of one of the expected token types as
  specified via the *select*, *unselect* or *expect* arguments.

ValueError: If one of the token types passed to *select*, *unselect* or *expect* is not a
  known token type.

class_variables.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer.log
documentation: type: ignore

class_variables.name: nr.util.parsing._tokenizer.tokenizer.Tokenizer.Debug
documentation: NOSONAR
Splits text from a #Scanner into #Token#s via rules provided by a #Lexer.
